item.title: Man facing ICE deportation to the Congo: "I feel like a person who has no value"
item.description: Jose Yugar-Cruz was granted a court order preventing his deportation to his home country, but the Trump administration is set to send him to the Congo.

item.title: ICE reports 18th detainee death in 2026, putting agency on track for new record
item.description: ICE reported the 18th death of an individual in its custody so far this year, putting the agency on track to record a new all-time high in detainee deaths.

item.title: Confrontation between officers and alleged shooter at D.C. gala lasted 7 seconds
item.description: Seven seconds passed between when the alleged gunman at Saturday's White House Correspondents' Dinner — carrying a shotgun initially concealed by a jacket — first encountered federal law enforcement and when he was subdued, sources told CBS News.

item.title: How the 76-day partial government shutdown ended
item.description: The 76-day partial government shutdown, which impacted funding for the Department of Homeland Security, has ended. CBS News' Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Iran says it has offered a new proposal for peace talks with the U.S.
item.description: Iran says it's offered a new proposal for talks with the U.S. to end the war, as the Strait of Hormuz standoff sends costs soaring around the world.

item.title: Iran sends U.S. revised proposal for talks to end war, Pakistani officials say
item.description: Iran delivered a revised response to the latest U.S. amendments on an agreement to end the war, Pakistan officials tell CBS News. Imtiaz Tyab reports.

item.title: How much will a $250,000 annuity pay monthly at age 60?
item.description: A $250,000 annuity can deliver a hefty amount each month at age 60, but the exact payout hinges on several factors.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (May 3)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Meet the horses running in the Kentucky Derby
item.description: The Kentucky Derby will see a full field of 20 horses in the first leg of the 2026 competition for horse racing's Triple Crown.

item.title: New White House drug abuse strategy floats wastewater testing, AI, more treatment
item.description: The Trump administration is proposing wastewater testing to try to ferret out data on illegal drug use in real time, according to a draft of a new drug control strategy obtained by CBS News. It also proposes using AI to track threats.

item.title: 2 employees killed in Kentucky bank robbery, person of interest in custody
item.description: Kentucky State Police said a man went to a U.S. Bank in Brea, Kentucky, and shot and killed a man and a woman, both employees at the bank.

item.title: More Americans are quitting their U.S. jobs to work abroad, study finds
item.description: Since 2021, the share of U.S.-based employees who have left their jobs to work in another country has more than doubled.

item.title: Artemis crew surprises 5-year-old aspiring astronaut with special gift
item.description: The Artemis II team gained a new member, and the crew made sure their youngest teammate had the right stuff for space.

item.title: What's next for the Artemis missions and potential return to the moon
item.description: Last month the Artemis II crew completed its historic mission, traveling to the far side of the moon. In 2028, NASA plans to send astronauts back to the moon. Mark Strassmann reports on what comes next for the Artemis missions.

item.title: Astronaut Christina Koch details broken toilet ordeal, "great" showers in space
item.description: Artemis astronaut Christina Koch explained the crew's broken toilet troubles and why she thinks showers in space "are great." The crew appeared on "CBS Mornings" for a CBS News town hall.

item.title: Artemis II crew surprises 5-year-old boy and answers true or false space questions
item.description: The Artemis II crew surprised a 5-year-old boy space enthusiast and answered "CBS Mornings" co-host Gayle King's true or false questions about burping in space and whether you become taller in space. The crew appeared for a CBS News town hall "Artemis II: A Celebration of Heroes."

item.title: Bill Nye demonstrates experiments that break down Artemis II mission
item.description: Bill Nye The Science Guy, the chief ambassador of The Planetary Society, joins "CBS Mornings" to demonstrate some experiments that break down the Artemis II mission.

item.title: Artemis crew tells kids moon flight was "the best roller coaster ride"
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts joined "CBS Mornings" for a live town hall where they took questions from kids just weeks after returning from their historic moon mission.

item.title: Ron Howard, Artemis astronauts joke about bathrooms while recalling "Apollo 13" film's impact
item.description: Ron Howard joined "CBS Mornings" to talk about directing his 11995 film, "Apollo 13," and the duality of watching the Artemis II astronauts' liftoff in April. The crew also shared how Howard's film impacted them, and Howard recalled a story where he told Kevin Bacon, "You get 10 extra close-ups if you keep your mouth shut!"

item.title: Artemis astronauts reveal the candy they ate immediately after splashdown
item.description: Artemis II astronauts revealed what they were munching on as they waited for rescue forces to open the Orion Capsule's hatch after splashdown. The bag of candy was in Christina Koch's spacesuit pocket.

item.title: As Iran war hits key 60-day deadline, Congress and Trump face choices
item.description: The War Powers Resolution sets deadlines for the president to end hostilities without congressional approval.

item.title: Amtrak may ease rules on guns on its trains, sources say
item.description: Amtrak may ease rules on guns on its trains, sources say. Critics worry that would weaken security even though, authorities say, the accused correspondents' dinner shooter took Amtrak cross-country with his firearms.

item.title: Artemis astronauts describe "spectacular" moon mission, eating peanut M&M's after splashdown
item.description: Artemis II astronauts joined "CBS Mornings" for a town hall, "Artemis II: A Celebration of Heroes." They described the moments before liftoff and splashdown, and the funny moment just after coming back to Earth that prompted "CBS Mornings" co-host to say, "astronauts, they're just like us."

item.title: Trump officials aboard as nonstop flights from U.S. to Venezuela resume
item.description: The trip comes nearly four months after U.S. forces seized Rodríguez's predecessor, Nicolás Maduro​, and his wife in a daring special forces raid.

item.title: "Ozempic breath" is boosting Hershey's sales of mints and gum
item.description: Hershey says it's benefiting from the growing use of GLP-1 weight-loss drugs even as people cut down on snacks. Here's why.

item.title: Details revealed on where Cole Allen is being held in D.C. jail complex
item.description: Cole Allen, 31, is charged with attempting to assassinate President Trump and two firearms-related offenses stemming from the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner on Saturday night.

item.title: Suspect in D.C. shooting held in "safe cell," agrees to remain jailed ahead of trial
item.description: Cole Allen, 31, is facing three charges related to the attack outside the White House Correspondents' Dinner, including attempting to assassinate President Trump.

item.title: U.K. pledges $34 million to fight antisemitism emergency after stabbing
item.description: The British government's terrorism prevention adviser describes anti-Jewish attacks as the "biggest national security emergency" since 2017.

item.title: Secret Service officer shot at gala was not hit by friendly fire, sources say
item.description: Federal investigators have also determined that the suspected gunman, 31-year-old Cole Allen, fired his shotgun, the sources said.

item.title: DOJ seeks pretrial detention of shooting suspect Cole Allen, shares new photos
item.description: Cole Allen was charged Monday in federal court with three counts, including attempting to assassinate the president. The other two charges involved the use and transport of firearms.
